name: Lor'kas Ik 577 (Ember)
description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis. The simplest are adapted to haul materials between refinery and factory stations. Fittingly, they're named after a productive colony of the Efreti, to honor their focus on peaceful industry.

name: Korath Warlord (Prototype)
description: After gaining access to Sestor and Mereti technology, the Exiles have developed this new ship. Unlike the Palavret or Rano'erek, this is a true war machine.

name: Model 8 (disruptor)
description: The primary role of the smallest Kor Mereti drones is asteroid mining, but their lasers can also be employed as offensive weapons when necessary.

name: Model 8 (minelayer)
description: The primary role of the smallest Kor Mereti drones is asteroid mining, but their lasers can also be employed as offensive weapons when necessary.

name: Korath Warlord (Artillery)
description: After gaining access to Sestor and Mereti technology, the Exiles have developed this new ship. Unlike the Palavret or Rano'erek, this is a true war machine.

name: Model 1024 (Greenhouse)
description: The next step in the evolution of the Kor Mereti war-machine is the Model 1024, somewhat improved beyond a perfect ring. Sporting minimal armament with a sudden conversion to a cargo hauling vessel, this was obviously developed after the Kor Mereti gained sapience and diverted their focus to less self-destructive endeavors.

name: Model 1024 (Infested)
description: The next step in the evolution of the Kor Mereti war-machine is the Model 1024, somewhat improved beyond a perfect ring. Sporting minimal armament with a sudden conversion to a cargo hauling vessel, this was obviously developed after the Kor Mereti gained sapience and diverted their focus to less self-destructive endeavors.

name: Korath Forager (Tormentor)
description: Originally designed as a military scout, the Kor Efret have repurposed these interceptors for mining asteroids.

name: Garbage Scow (Smuggler)
description: The Garbage Scow is an ancient ship: developed hundreds of years ago to transport radioactive materials across human space, it had lost importance with the introduction of generalised and better armed haulers like the Bulk Freighter and the Behemoth. Some still operate legitimately, carrying waste away to designated disposal sites. But many of these freighters have wound up in the hands of smugglers after the discovery that the internal structure of their Radiological Containment cylinders are very efficient at hiding cargo from being scanned.

name: Garbage Scow (Jammer)
description: The Garbage Scow is an ancient ship: developed hundreds of years ago to transport radioactive materials across human space, it had lost importance with the introduction of generalised and better armed haulers like the Bulk Freighter and the Behemoth. Some still operate legitimately, carrying waste away to designated disposal sites. But many of these freighters have wound up in the hands of smugglers after the discovery that the internal structure of their Radiological Containment cylinders are very efficient at hiding cargo from being scanned.

name: Garbage Scow (Stinger)
description: The Garbage Scow is an ancient ship: developed hundreds of years ago to transport radioactive materials across human space, it had lost importance with the introduction of generalised and better armed haulers like the Bulk Freighter and the Behemoth. Some still operate legitimately, carrying waste away to designated disposal sites. But many of these freighters have wound up in the hands of smugglers after the discovery that the internal structure of their Radiological Containment cylinders are very efficient at hiding cargo from being scanned.

Missions: Midnight Expansion: Bounty Hunting (Boarding, Stinger Missile)

description: The Garbage Scow is an ancient ship: developed hundreds of years ago to transport radioactive materials across human space, it had lost importance with the introduction of generalised and better armed haulers like the Bulk Freighter and the Behemoth. Some still operate legitimately, carrying waste away to designated disposal sites. But many of these freighters have wound up in the hands of smugglers after the discovery that the internal structure of their Radiological Containment cylinders are very efficient at hiding cargo from being scanned.

name: Modified Garbage Scow (Heavy)
description: When the Radiological Containment cylinders on a Garbage Scow have been damaged beyond repair, removing them is often easier than finding a replacement. This transforms the Garbage Scow into a compact medium warship.

name: Modified Garbage Scow (Proton)
description: When the Radiological Containment cylinders on a Garbage Scow have been damaged beyond repair, removing them is often easier than finding a replacement. This transforms the Garbage Scow into a compact medium warship.

name: Modified Garbage Scow (Quad Blaster)
description: When the Radiological Containment cylinders on a Garbage Scow have been damaged beyond repair, removing them is often easier than finding a replacement. This transforms the Garbage Scow into a compact medium warship.

name: Rukh (Energy Cannons)
description: A Firebird-based ship design produced at the Wei Shipyards centuries ago, the Rukh was a vessel that qualifies as a middle ground between medium and heavy warships. Betelgeuse Shipyards sued their competitor at Wei for patent infringement shortly after the Republic was formed. The court judgement left Wei Shipyards bankrupt and production of the Rukh abruptly ceased.

name: Rukh (Turbo Blaster)
description: A Firebird-based ship design produced at the Wei Shipyards centuries ago, the Rukh was a vessel that qualifies as a middle ground between medium and heavy warships. Betelgeuse Shipyards sued their competitor at Wei for patent infringement shortly after the Republic was formed. The court judgement left Wei Shipyards bankrupt and production of the Rukh abruptly ceased.

name: Rukh (Turbo)
description: A Firebird-based ship design produced at the Wei Shipyards centuries ago, the Rukh was a vessel that qualifies as a middle ground between medium and heavy warships. Betelgeuse Shipyards sued their competitor at Wei for patent infringement shortly after the Republic was formed. The court judgement left Wei Shipyards bankrupt and production of the Rukh abruptly ceased.

Missions: Midnight Expansion: FW Prisoner Parole Add On

name: Rukh (Heavy)
description: A Firebird-based ship design produced at the Wei Shipyards centuries ago, the Rukh was a vessel that qualifies as a middle ground between medium and heavy warships. Betelgeuse Shipyards sued their competitor at Wei for patent infringement shortly after the Republic was formed. The court judgement left Wei Shipyards bankrupt and production of the Rukh abruptly ceased.

name: Marauder Rukh (Inferno Rocket)
description: After the discovery of the original blueprints for the Rukh, mechanics were able to combine that data with modern ship design principles to further optimize the design for combat.

name: Marauder Rukh (Heavy)
description: After the discovery of the original blueprints for the Rukh, mechanics were able to combine that data with modern ship design principles to further optimize the design for combat.

name: Water Scorpion (Jump)
description: The Water Scorpion was an ancient strike vessel that Hai warlords deployed to patrol their conquered territories. They declined in popularity with the rise of the Shield Beetle. Centuries later, memories of this ship would inspire the Unfettered to design the Sea Scorpion.

name: Water Scorpion (Jump, Tracker)
description: The Water Scorpion was an ancient strike vessel that Hai warlords deployed to patrol their conquered territories. They declined in popularity with the rise of the Shield Beetle. Centuries later, memories of this ship would inspire the Unfettered to design the Sea Scorpion.

name: Water Scorpion (Tracker)
description: The Water Scorpion was an ancient strike vessel that Hai warlords deployed to patrol their conquered territories. They declined in popularity with the rise of the Shield Beetle. Centuries later, memories of this ship would inspire the Unfettered to design the Sea Scorpion.

name: Korath World Crafter (Kor Efret World Crafter)
sprite: ship/Midnight PR4987 korath refuge/Midnight PR4987 korath refuge
description: The Korath World Crafter manufactures parts, medicine, medical equipment, and sufficient food for bare sustenance. Onboard, there are incubators, nurseries, and hospitals.

name: Anomalocaris (Shock Wave)
description: First created during the Hai's initial conflicts with the Sheragi, the Anomalocaris was supposedly one of the most versatile Hai warships of its time. Its high cargo capacity and low engine space suggest the original purpose of the ship was for mining or hauling, though it could also have found use in looting enemy ships and carrying ammunition. Vessels from the time were manufactured out of more expensive materials, making the Anomalocaris more sturdy and able to take larger amounts of damage.

name: Cicada (Shock Wave)
description: A design nearly as old as the Aphid, practically no new Cicadas have been built in recent memory; it is so reliable that the second-hand market is saturated with Cicadas that have outlived not just one pilot, but in many cases whole lineages of Hai that have owned one and passed it down through the generations. This feature is likely the main factor which has kept the popularity of the Scarab in check as well as defining the Yeer e Ki shipyard's motto: "Service for Life."

name: Ladybug (Shock Wave)
description: These sensor-laden exploration vessels hail back to a more peaceful era of the Hai. Though they have no true ramscoop, a technology the Hai never developed, their built-in collection gills magnify the natural ramscoop present in all ships. These expensive features are the reason the ship has been replaced with the Lightning Bug. However, this ship has seen combat in recent years against human pirates. With room for plentiful shield regeneration, good maneuverability, and the range to strike deep in pirate territory, their long sleep has ended.

name: Lightning Bug (Afterburner)
description: The Lightning Bug is a small defensive ship, mostly used by the Hai as a convoy escort. The Lightning Bug, as with many insects, works best in a swarm: even a small number of Lightning Bugs can manage to leave the largest of ships helpless with their stock-fitted Ion Cannons.

name: Lightning Bug (Shock Wave)
description: The Lightning Bug is a small defensive ship, mostly used by the Hai as a convoy escort. The Lightning Bug, as with many insects, works best in a swarm: even a small number of Lightning Bugs can manage to leave the largest of ships helpless with their stock-fitted Ion Cannons.

name: Sea Scorpion (Pulse Repeater Afterburner)
description: The Sea Scorpion was designed several decades ago to serve as a mobile platform to launch Trackers. However, it is only recently that the Unfettered have been able to mass-produce them. While individually vulnerable, the reduced cost of manufacturing compared to Shield Beetles allow the materially inferior Unfettered to gain a numerical advantage against their brethren.

name: Sea Scorpion (Pulse Afterburner)
description: The Sea Scorpion was designed several decades ago to serve as a mobile platform to launch Trackers. However, it is only recently that the Unfettered have been able to mass-produce them. While individually vulnerable, the reduced cost of manufacturing compared to Shield Beetles allow the materially inferior Unfettered to gain a numerical advantage against their brethren.

name: Sea Scorpion (Shock Wave)
description: The Sea Scorpion was designed several decades ago to serve as a mobile platform to launch Trackers. However, it is only recently that the Unfettered have been able to mass-produce them. While individually vulnerable, the reduced cost of manufacturing compared to Shield Beetles allow the materially inferior Unfettered to gain a numerical advantage against their brethren.

name: Shield Beetle (Accelerator Collector)
description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

name: Shield Beetle (Pulse Afterburner)
description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

name: Shield Beetle (Jump, Pulse Repeater Afterburner)
description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

name: Shield Beetle (Shock Wave Ion)
description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

name: Solifuge (Shock Wave)
description: Impressed by the Alphas' "Giftbringer," the Unfettered endeavored to build their own fighter carrier to take more advantage of their supply of jump drives.

name: Violin Spider (Shock Wave)
description: The Hai fighter is equipped with what most humans would consider the greatest technology available, but all of that high tech comes at a cost. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

name: Water Bug (Shock Wave)
description: The design of the Water Bug is almost as old as the Aphid, but until recently it never saw much use. A spike in aggression from the Unfettered Hai has led the tougher and more well-defended Water Bug to see an increase in popularity over its little brother, the Aphid. The Water Bug is now the most widely used Hai freighter, comprising the bulk of all Hai merchant fleets.

name: Arch-Carrack (Shocker)
description: Although they do not effectively provide for their own defense, the Efreti remain responsible for the day-to-day needs of civilian and cargo transportation. While not as bulky or powerful as other Korath designs, this freighter is much faster at making runs back and forth between the Efreti's handful of remaining planets, while still holding enough defensive loadout to ward off the odd Mereti drone that slips past the Quarg.

name: Charm-Shallop (Shocker)
description: A recent design, this Efreti multi-role vessel is found taking up many jobs previously relegated to a now scrapped fleet of early-model raiders. While some are used primarily for passenger transport, others are used in local mining to satiate the Efreti's perpetual demand for ores. Incorporating Quarg-inspired structural elements, its light frame allows it to complete tasks more nimbly than any other independent Korath ship.

name: Echo-Galleon (Shocker)
description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis, including this large Efreti vessel. Facing very different challenges, and taking some inspiration from their Quarg protectors, the Efreti descendant of the "World-Ships" of old is lighter, faster, and more efficient, but less powerful than its Exile counterparts.

name: Korath World-Ship (Shocker)
description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

name: Korath World-Ship B (Shocker)
description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

name: Korath World-Ship C (Shocker)
description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

name: Korath Scouter (Crippled)
description: The Sku'uta is a Korath reconnaissance ship intended for quick forays into hostile territory.

name: Korath Scouter (Repeater)
description: The Sku'uta is a Korath reconnaissance ship intended for quick forays into hostile territory.

name: Korath Scouter (Fire-Lance)
description: The Sku'uta is a Korath reconnaissance ship intended for quick forays into hostile territory.

name: Korsmanath A'awoj (Refiner)
description: Korsmanath A'awojs, named in memory of the Korath home system, are massive nomadic habitats that hold much of their remaining population. True flying cities, they could be self-contained miniature worlds were it not for their heavy defensive armament and reliance on unsustainable materials, methods, and sources of power. 	Commission of new Korsmanath A'awojs is a resource-demanding, labor-intensive, painstaking process fraught with political intrigue. Navarchs fight with the civilian government for resources and yard time they would rather devote to building raiding and defensive fleets, civilians vie with each other for a place on the next ship, and planning committees argue with engineers over what methods and technologies to use. This leads to construction delays, corner-cutting, and over-militarization of the design at best, and violent feuds at worst.

name: Korath World-Ship (Shock-Strike)
description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

name: Korath World-Ship B (Shock-Strike)
description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

name: Korath World-Ship C (Shock-Strike)
description: Korath World-Ships are massive nomadic habitats that hold all that is left of their people.

name: Avialae (Disarmed)
description: The Avialae is an early example of Remnant ship development. Its design function still has traces of principles from ancestral human space. As they acclimated to life in the Ember Wastes many Prefects determined that mass production of big Utility ships was a waste of resources. Instead they chose to focus on filling their fleets with smaller, more specialized, models.

name: Ekfar Tek 23 (Salvaged)
description: This freighter is used by new Kor Sestor colonies to harvest and transport raw materials.

name: Korbatar Makwar (Exile Upgrade)
description: One of the last great Korath battleships from the height of the war that drove their species to near extinction, the Korbatar Makwar is an engine of destruction that dominates conventional battlefields. 	The number of these battleships which are still in operation can be counted on one hand. Korath shipyards are fully capable of assembling more Korbatar Makwars, but the exile from Korath space has forced them to prioritize construction of Korsmanath A'awojs and other support vessels instead. A small, but vocal, group of Navarchs continue to lobby various planning committees for construction of new Korbatar Makwars. But the civilian government refuses to capitulate to their demands.

name: Korbatar Makwar (Efret Upgrade)
description: One of the last great Korath battleships from the height of the war that drove their species to near extinction, the Korbatar Makwar is an engine of destruction that dominates conventional battlefields. 	The number of these battleships which are still in operation can be counted on one hand. Korath shipyards are fully capable of assembling more Korbatar Makwars, but the exile from Korath space has forced them to prioritize construction of Korsmanath A'awojs and other support vessels instead. A small, but vocal, group of Navarchs continue to lobby various planning committees for construction of new Korbatar Makwars. But the civilian government refuses to capitulate to their demands.

name: Modified Cargo Star (Smuggler)
description: The Cargo Star was a proprietary freighter used by Starwell Shipping Corporation before it was bought out by the Syndicate in a hostile corporate takeover. Many of the technologies and design principles from this ship can be found in the modern designs of Syndicate Shipyards. 	Most Cargo Stars that survived the Alpha Wars did so by having extensive modifications made to strengthen their hull, shields, and weapon capacity.

name: Magpie (Grave Digger)
description: Shortly after development of the Starling, Remnant ship crafters attempted to apply the design principles they learned towards a medium warship. The Magpie was one functional result of this endeavor. 	There was an unforeseen problem with trying to apply the alien cloaking technology they had access to with a bigger ship: a noticeable increase in consumption of energy and fuel. Until the shipyards solve this problem, larger ships with cloaking will remain a rare sight in Remnant fleets. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Magpie's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

Missions: Midnight Expansion Nemesis Grave Digger Magpies
